title: मुकुन्दमाला स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री मुकुन्दमाला स्तोत्रम् ॥

॥ कुलशेखर आळ्वार विरचितम् ॥

श्रीवल्लभेति वरदेति दयापरेति
भक्तप्रियेति भवलुण्ठनकोविदेति।
नाथेति नागशयनेति जगन्निवासे-
त्यालापनं प्रतिपदं कुरु मे मुकुन्द॥ १॥

जयतु जयतु देवो देवकीनन्दनोऽयं
जयतु जयतु कृष्णो वृष्णिवंशप्रदीपः।
जयतु जयतु मेघश्यामलः कोमलाङ्गो
जयतु जयतु पृथ्वीभारनाशो मुकुन्दः॥ २॥

मुकुन्द मूर्ध्ना प्रणिपत्य याचे
भवन्तमेकान्तमियन्तमर्थम्।
अविस्मृतिस्त्वच्चरणारविन्दे
भवे भवे मेऽस्तु भवत्प्रसादात्॥ ३॥

नाहं वन्दे तव चरणयोर्द्वन्द्वमद्वन्द्वहेतोः
कुम्भीपाकं गुरुमपि हरे नारकं नापनेतुम्।
रम्यारामामृदुतनुलता नन्दने नापि रन्तुं
भावे भावे हृदयभवने भावयेयं भवन्तम्॥ ४॥

नास्था धर्मे न वसुनिचये नैव कामोपभोगे
यद्यद्भव्यं भवतु भगवन् पूर्वकर्मानुरूपम्।
एतत्प्रार्थ्यं मम बहुमतं जन्मजन्मान्तरेऽपि
त्वत्पादाम्भोरुहयुगगता निश्चला भक्तिरस्तु॥ ५॥

दिवि वा भुवि वा ममास्तु वासो
नरके वा नरकान्तक प्रकामम्।
अवधीरित शारदारविन्दौ
चरणौ ते मरणेऽपि चिन्तयामि॥ ६॥

कृष्ण त्वदीयपदपङ्कजपञ्जरान्ते
अद्यैव मे विशतु मानसराजहंसः।
प्राणप्रयाणसमये कफवातपित्तैः
कण्ठावरोधनविधौ स्मरणं कुतस्ते॥ ७॥

चिन्तयामि हरिमेव सनातनं
मन्दमन्दहसिताननपङ्कजम्।
नन्दगोपतनयं परमादरं
नारदादिमुनिबृन्दवन्दितम्॥ ८॥

करचरणसरोजे कान्तिमन्नेत्रमीने
श्रवणयुगलझषे स्पर्शवत्सौष्ठवाढ्ये।
हृदयसरसि लीलालोलमत्स्यं विहर्तुं
म्म समुदयतु मने मानसाम्भोजहंसम्॥ ९॥

भवजलधिगतानां द्वन्द्ववाताहतानां
सुतदुहितृकलत्रत्राणभारार्दितानाम्।
विषमविषयतोये मज्जतामप्लवानां
भवतु शरणमेको विष्णुपोतो नराणाम्॥ १०॥

मज्जन्मनः फलमिदं मधुकैटभारे
मत्प्रार्थनीयमदमर्थमपि ध्रुवं ते।
त्वत्पादकम्लजयितोदरसंनिविष्टा
भक्तिर्दृढा भगवति प्रतिकल्पमेषा॥ ११॥

॥ इति श्री कुलशेखर आळ्वार विरचितं मुकुन्दमाला स्तोत्रम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Mukunda Mala Stotram ||

|| Kulashekhara Alwar Virachitam ||

Shri-vallabheti Varadeti Dayapareti
Bhakta-priyeti Bhava-lunthana-kovideti.
Natheti Naga-shayaneti Jagan-nivase-
tyalapanam pratipadam kuru me Mukunda. || 1 ||

Jayatu jayatu devo Devaki-nandano-ayam
jayatu jayatu Krishno Vrishni-vamsha-pradipah.
Jayatu jayatu megha-shyamalah komalango
jayatu jayatu prithvi-bhara-nasho Mukundah. || 2 ||

Mukunda murdhna pranipaty yache
bhavantam-ekantam-iyantam-artham.
Avismritis-tvach-charana-aravinde
bhave bhave me-astu bhavat-prasadat. || 3 ||

Naham vande tava charanor-dvandvam-advandva-hetoh
Kumbhipakam gurum-api Hare narakam na-apanetum.
Ramya-arama-mridu-tanu-lata Nandane na-api rantum
bhave bhave hridaya-bhavane bhavayeyam bhavantam. || 4 ||

Nastha dharme na vasu-nichaye naiva kamopabhoge
yad-yad-bhavyam bhavatu Bhagavan purva-karma-anurupam.
Etat-prarthyam mama bahumatam janma-janmantare-api
tvat-padambhoruha-yuga-gata nishchala bhaktir-astu. || 5 ||

Divi va bhuvi va mama-astu vaso
narake va Narakantaka prakamam.
Avadhirita Sharada-aravindau
charanau te marane-api chintayami. || 6 ||

Krishna tvadiya-pada-pankaja-panjarantam
adyaiva me vishatu manasa-raja-hamsah.
Prana-prayana-samaye kapha-vata-pittaih
kantha-avarodhana-vidhau smaranam kutaste. || 7 ||

Chintayami Harim-eva sanatanam
manda-manda-hasita-anana-pankajam.
Nanda-gopa-tanayam parama-adaram
Naradadi-muni-brinda-vanditam. || 8 ||

Kara-charana-saroje kanti-man-netra-mine
shravana-yugala-jhashe sparsha-vat-saushthava-adhye.
Hridaya-sarasi lila-lola-matsyam vihartum
mama samudayatu mane mansa-ambhoja-hamsam. || 9 ||

Bhava-jaladhi-gatanam dvandva-vata-ahatanam
suta-duhitr-kalatra-trana-bhara-arditanam.
Vishama-vishaya-toye majjatam-aplavanam
bhavatu sharanam-eko Vishnu-poto naranam. || 10 ||

Maj-janmanah phalam-idam Madhu-Kaitabhare
mat-prarthaniyam-adam-artham-api dhruvam te.
Tvat-pada-kamala-jayit-odara-sannivishta
bhaktir-dridha Bhagavati pratikalpam-esha. || 11 ||

|| Iti Shri Kulashekhara Alwar Virachitam Mukunda Mala Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
हे मुकुन्द! श्रीवल्लभ, वरद, दयापर, भक्तप्रिय, भवनाशक, नाथ, नागशयन, जगन्निवास - ऐसे प्रतिपद आलापन मुझसे करवाओ॥ १॥

जय हो जय हो देवकीनन्दन की! जय हो कृष्ण वृष्णिवंश के दीपक की! जय हो मेघश्यामल कोमलांग की! जय हो पृथ्वी का भार हरने वाले मुकुन्द की!॥ २॥

हे मुकुन्द! मैं मस्तक झुकाकर एक ही वर माँगता हूँ - आपके चरण कमलों का विस्मरण जन्म-जन्मान्तर में कभी न हो॥ ३॥

हे हरि! मैं आपके चरणों की वन्दना द्वन्द्व निवारण या कुम्भीपाक नरक से बचने या नन्दनवन में रमण के लिए नहीं करता। मैं तो जन्म-जन्म में हृदय मन्दिर में आपका ही ध्यान करना चाहता हूँ॥ ४॥

न धर्म में आस्था, न धन संचय में, न काम उपभोग में। जो होना है हे भगवन, पूर्व कर्मानुसार हो। मेरी एक ही प्रार्थना है - जन्म-जन्मान्तर में भी आपके चरण कमलों में अचल भक्ति रहे॥ ५॥

स्वर्ग में, पृथ्वी पर या नरक में कहीं भी मेरा वास हो, हे नरकान्तक! शरद ऋतु के कमलों को भी लज्जित करने वाले आपके चरणों का मरण काल में भी मैं चिन्तन करूँगा॥ ६॥

हे कृष्ण! आज ही मेरा मानस राजहंस आपके चरण कमल रूपी पञ्जर में प्रवेश कर ले, क्योंकि प्राण निकलने के समय कफ-वात-पित्त से कण्ठ अवरुद्ध होने पर आपका स्मरण कहाँ से होगा!॥ ७॥
benefits: Deepens pure devotion (bhakti) towards Lord Krishna/Mukunda
Invokes unwavering remembrance of god's lotus feet
Traditionally recited for removal of attachment to worldly pleasures
Traditionally recited to invoke divine remembrance even at the time of death
Invokes the blessing of liberation across multiple lifetimes
Rescues devotees from the ocean of samsara
description: The Mukunda Mala Stotram, known in Sanskrit as मुकुन्दमाला स्तोत्रम्, is a devotional hymn of praise in praise of Lord Krishna. Drawn from Kulashekhara Alwar, this text has been cherished by devotees for centuries. Devotees recite this prayer to bestow liberation across multiple lifetimes and ensure divine remembrance even at the time of death. It is also said to rescue devotees from the ocean of samsara. This prayer is traditionally recited on Wednesday (Budhvar) and Thursday (Guruvar) and during Janmashtami and Ekadashi to invoke divine grace. With 11 verses and a reading time of about 12 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Mukunda Mala Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Mukunda Mala Stotram by Kulashekhara Alwar in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Garland of devotion to Lord Mukunda for supreme bhakti.

seoFaqs.question: Who composed the Mukunda Mala Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: The Mukunda Mala was composed by Kulashekhara Alvar (also called Kulashekhara Perumal), the seventh of the twelve Alvars of the Sri Vaishnava bhakti tradition of Tamil Nadu, who is traditionally dated to around the 9th century CE. He was a Chera king who renounced his throne to live the life of a wandering devotee of Vishnu. The Mukundamala is one of his two principal compositions, the other being the Tamil Perumal Tirumozhi. Although the Mukundamala is in Sanskrit, its bhava is unmistakably Alvar prapatti (surrender) bhakti, which is why it is anthologised in both Sri Vaishnava and Gaudiya Vaishnava liturgies, the latter through Chaitanya tradition's reverence for the Alvars.

seoFaqs.question: How many verses does the Mukunda Mala actually contain?
seoFaqs.answer: The verse count differs across recensions. The Wikipedia entry and several traditional Sri Vaishnava editions count forty verses. The recension popularised through the Gaudiya Vaishnava tradition, including the Bhaktivedanta Vedabase edition, counts fifty-three verses, including additional benedictory and prapatti shlokas. Both counts agree on the core Kulashekhara verses; the divergence lies in which closing verses are treated as integral and which are seen as later supplications added by devotees. Pages should ideally state both numbers and clarify which recension is being referenced. The phala-shruti is consistent: whoever recites the Mukunda Mala has all sinful reactions destroyed and reaches Vishnu's supreme abode.

seoFaqs.question: What is the central theme of the Mukunda Mala Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: The central theme is sharanagati, total surrender to Krishna under his name Mukunda, the giver of mukti. Kulashekhara repeatedly asks Krishna for one boon only: undeviating remembrance of him, especially at the moment of death, regardless of any other circumstance. He prays to be reborn even as a low creature if necessary, provided his devotion to Krishna remains unbroken. The hymn also dwells on the misery of samsara, the unreliability of worldly attachments, and the conviction that Krishna alone is the means and the goal. This intensity of one-pointed devotion makes the Mukunda Mala a foundational text of prapatti bhakti within the Sri Vaishnava and broader Vaishnava traditions.

seoFaqs.question: Why is the Mukunda Mala important in the Sri Vaishnava tradition?
seoFaqs.answer: The Mukunda Mala holds a unique position because it bridges the Tamil Alvar bhakti tradition and the Sanskrit Vedanta tradition. Kulashekhara's Tamil Perumal Tirumozhi entered the Divya Prabandham (the Tamil Veda of the Sri Vaishnavas), while his Mukunda Mala in Sanskrit became accessible to the pan-Indian Vedantic audience. Ramanujacharya and later acharyas of Sri Vaishnavism cite Kulashekhara as a model of prapatti, and the Mukunda Mala is included in the daily anushthana of many Sri Vaishnava households. Its inclusion in Gaudiya Vaishnava reading lists by Chaitanya followers further established its cross-sampradaya importance among Vishnu devotees.

seoFaqs.question: When is the Mukunda Mala traditionally recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The most prescribed days are every Ekadashi tithi, Janmashtami, Vaikuntha Ekadashi (in Margashirsha-Pausha), Gita Jayanti, the birthday-tithi of Kulashekhara Alvar (commemorated in Sri Vaishnava temples), and during the annual Adhyayanotsavam at Srirangam and other divya-deshams. For nitya-parayana, devotees recite the full text once daily or break it into seven sections to complete weekly. It is also commonly chanted at the bedside of the dying and during shraddha rites because of its repeated invocation of Krishna's name at antyakala. The hymn is short enough that a careful recitation takes about 25 to 35 minutes depending on the recension.
